concept: Sage / Wise One / Elder of Wisdom
description: One possessing profound wisdom; a person whose insight comes from deep experience and reflection. 'Phewo' names who sees truly—wisdom earned through living.
sound_symbolism: The word breathes with knowing. 'Phe' whispers with quiet wisdom, knowledge distilled. 'Wo' wonders with profound seeing, truth perceived.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo phewo' (the sage). Beyond 'thonua' (scholar)—wisdom rather than knowledge. The goal of 'thole' (practice) over a lifetime.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sage as awakened—one who has realized through practice what others only study.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sage as elder—wisdom that comes from long life lived attentively.
